meta.title: Osteopath in Avignon — Matthieu Yeghiazarian D.O. (English)
meta.description: English-speaking osteopath D.O. in Avignon, France. 16 years of experience. Adults, athletes, pregnancy, infants and children. Online booking via Doctolib.

hero.subtitle: Hands-on, holistic osteopathy tailored to each patient. 16 years of experience. Consultations in English and French, by appointment.

movement.title: Movement
movement.body: Movement is at the heart of osteopathy. Each session focuses on how the body moves as a whole, identifying restrictions and restoring mobility — for movement that feels easier, more fluid, more natural every day.

precision.title: Precision
precision.body: Osteopathic care relies on careful observation and precise, hands-on techniques. Every treatment is adapted to the individual, with close attention to posture, balance, and the way the body responds.

performance.title: Performance
performance.body: Performance isn't just about sport. It's how well the body supports daily life, work, recovery, and physical activity. Osteopathy helps the body function efficiently — for athletic demands and everyday movement alike.

services.title: Osteopathy for every stage of life

adults.title: Adults & active life
adults.body: Neck, back, and lower-back pain. Tendinopathies. Recovery from injuries. Postural strain from desk work or repetitive movement.

sport.title: Athletes
sport.body: Preparation before a competition, recovery after training, injury prevention, and movement quality. Tailored to your discipline.

pregnancy.title: Pregnancy & post-partum
pregnancy.body: Care through all three trimesters and after delivery: lower-back pain, sciatica, digestive discomfort, and postural recovery.

pediatric.title: Infants & children
pediatric.body: Gentle, age-appropriate hands-on approach, complementary to the child's medical follow-up. See the dedicated infants & children page.

seniors.title: Seniors
seniors.body: Mobility maintenance, fall prevention, support for chronic joint pain and post-surgery recovery.

english.title: English speakers
english.body: Full consultations in English for international patients, expats, and tourists visiting Avignon. Online booking via Doctolib.

about.title: A practice grounded in experience
about.lead: Matthieu Yeghiazarian, osteopath D.O., draws on more than 16 years of experience caring for his patients.
about.body_1: A graduate of the Collège d'Ostéopathie de Provence (Aix-Marseille), he has practised in France and abroad with patients of every age and walk of life. His work is grounded in precise hands-on technique and a holistic approach, built on a deep understanding of anatomy, biomechanics, and how the body works.
about.body_2: Outside the practice, Matthieu trains in Brazilian jiu-jitsu — a demanding discipline that fuels his interest in movement, biomechanics, and performance.

pricing.title: Transparent, no surprises
pricing.note: Cash or card accepted. A receipt is issued after each session for reimbursement by your private insurance. Osteopathy is not covered by the French Social Security.

testimonials.title: What patients say
testimonials.intro: Public reviews from the practice's Google Business Profile. Click through to verify them — or to leave your own.
testimonials.rating_summary: {average} out of 5 · {count, plural, =1 {# review} other {# reviews}} on Google

items.a: A consultation lasts 45 minutes on average. The first session may be slightly longer because it includes a full intake about your history, lifestyle, and reasons for the visit.
Sessions are €60 regardless of the reason for visit. A receipt is provided so you can claim reimbursement from your private health insurance.

items.q: Is osteopathy reimbursed by Social Security?
items.a: No, osteopathy is not covered by French Social Security. Most private insurance plans (mutuelles) cover all or part of the sessions, typically 2 to 6 per year.
No. Osteopaths in France are first-line practitioners — you can consult directly. If needed, the osteopath will refer you to another health professional.

items.q: From what age can someone consult?
items.a: From birth. For infants and children, the approach is exclusively gentle and hands-on, complementary to medical follow-up. Osteopathy does not replace medical advice: for any persistent symptoms in your child, please consult your primary care physician or pediatrician first.

items.q: How many sessions are typically needed?
items.a: For an acute issue, 1 to 3 sessions are usually enough. For chronic or preventive follow-up, 2 to 4 sessions per year is a good rhythm.

items.q: Do you offer consultations in English?
items.a: Yes. Matthieu speaks fluent English and welcomes English-speaking patients — tourists, expats, and international visitors. Online booking via Doctolib.

footer.intro: Osteopathy practice in the heart of Avignon. Holistic, hands-on care — in English and French.
